prs.title: [ROCm] Fallback SwigluOAI activation when _C op is absent
prs.author: Bortlesboat
prs.body_head:
## Summary
- add the same missing-`_C` fallback pattern to `SwigluOAIAndMul`
- switch `forward_cuda()` to use `self.op` so the class can cleanly fall back to `forward_native`
- add a focused regression test for ROCm/cuda-alike builds where `_C.swigluoai_and_mul` is absent

## Why this is not duplicating an existing PR
- I checked the live PR/issue surface for `swigluoai_and_mul` fallback work and did not find an open or merged PR covering this activation.
- This is a narrow follow-up to #39729, which applies the missing-`_C` fallback pattern to neighboring activations but does not touch `SwigluOAIAndMul`.

## Tests run
- `uv run --no-project --python 3.12 --with torch python -` with a stub loader around the real `custom_op.py` and `activation.py`
  - before the fix: reproduced `AttributeError: 'types.SimpleNamespace' object has no attribute 'swigluoai_and_mul'`
  - after the fix: `PASS`
- `uv run --no-project --python 3.12 python -`
  - `py_compile` succeeded for:
    - `vllm/model_executor/layers/activation.py`
    - `tests/kernels/core/test_activation.py`

## AI assistance
This PR was prepared with AI assistance.

prs.title: [ROCm] Fix UnboundLocalError for prefix_scheduler_metadata in cascade attention
prs.author: Bortlesboat
prs.body_head:
In `RocmAttentionMetadataBuilder.build()`, `prefix_scheduler_metadata` is only assigned inside the `else` branch (when `use_cascade=False`). When `use_cascade=True`, it's never set but passed to `RocmAttentionMetadata()` at line 156, which would raise `UnboundLocalError`.

Currently masked because `use_cascade_attention()` returns `False`, but this will crash the moment cascade is enabled for ROCM_ATTN (e.g., when prefix caching kicks in with shared prefixes).

Fix: initialize `prefix_scheduler_metadata = None` before the branch, consistent with how the other cascade variables are handled.

No duplicate PRs found. AI assistance was used for code search; all changes reviewed by human submitter.

Signed-off-by: Bortlesboat <bortstheboat@gmail.com>

prs.title: [ROCm][Bugfix] Fall back when Quark MoE AITER dispatch is unsupported
prs.author: Bortlesboat
prs.body_head:
Fixes #40008.

This catches the Quark OCP MX AITER MoE runtime error `Unsupported kernel config for moe heuristic dispatch` and falls back to the existing emulation path instead of aborting the run.

Why this is not duplicating an existing PR:
- `gh issue view 40008 --repo vllm-project/vllm --comments`
- `gh pr list --repo vllm-project/vllm --state open --search "40008 in:body"` returned no matches.
- `gh pr list --repo vllm-project/vllm --state open --search "Unsupported kernel config for moe heuristic dispatch"` returned no matches.
- `gh pr list --repo vllm-project/vllm --state open --search "moe heuristic dispatch rocm"` only found #38665, which is a different ROCm lane.

Tests run:
- `wsl.exe bash -lc "cd /mnt/c/Users/andre/.config/superpowers/worktrees/vllm/vllm-rocm-moe-heuristic-40008 && PYTHONPATH=/mnt/c/Users/andre/.config/superpowers/worktrees/vllm/vllm-rocm-moe-heuristic-40008 uv run --no-project --python 3.12 --with torch==2.11.0 --with pytest --with-requirements requirements/common.txt --with-requirements requirements/test/cuda.in python -m pytest tests/model_executor/layers/test_quark_ocp_mx_moe.py -q"` -> `2 passed`
- `wsl.exe bash -lc "cd /mnt/c/Users/andre/.config/superpowers/worktrees/vllm/vllm-rocm-moe-heuristic-40008 && uv run --no-project --python 3.12 --with ruff ruff check vllm/model_executor/layers/quantization/quark/quark_moe.py tests/model_executor/layers/test_quark_ocp_mx_moe.py"` -> `All checks passed!`

AI assistance: I used Codex to help draft the patch and regression test, then reviewed the diff and local verification before opening this PR.

prs.title: [vLLM IR] 2/N fused_add_rms_norm and maybe_inplace overload
prs.author: ProExpertProg
prs.body_head:
# [vLLM IR] 2/N Add maybe_inplace overload for in-place operation support

## Summary
This PR extends the vLLM IR framework to support optional in-place semantics 
(reusing input activation memory for outputs) via a new `maybe_inplace` overload.
It includes dispatching/registration infrastructure and additional compiler passes
to manage functionalization through the compilation pipeline.
Finally, this PR ports the `fused_add_rms_norm` to IR using the `maybe_inplace` overload.

Key features:
- **`maybe_inplace` overload**: Ops can allow in-place implementations to reuse activation tensor memory
- **eager-mode functionalization**: When the default overload dispatches to an inplace implementation,
activations are cloned to preserve their contents
- **E2E Compilation pipeline integration**: New and existing passes manage IR ops through the compilation pipeline, 
from functionalization to lowering and clone elimination.
- **Fusion passes updated**: Now using the `fused_add_rms_norm` IR op instead of 
- **IR documentation**: Added a full doc on all vLLM IR features: registration, dispatching, compilation, and overall design.
- **Unit & integration tests**: IR infra, kernels, compilation, etc.

## Motivation

vLLM IR separates operation **semantics** from **implementation**, enabling multiple implementations per op.
However, some kernels might optimize memory usage by reusing input activation buffers for outputs.
This poses two problems: (1) torch.compile does not like in-place operations, 
and (2) not all implementations of the IR op will have the same behavior.

To solve this, we introduce the `maybe_inplace` overload, which is allowed (but not required)
to alias output tensors to inputs. When a user calls the `maybe_inplace` overload, it signals
to the dispatcher & compiler that the inputs are no longer needed and their memory may be reused for outputs.

## Changes

### Core IR (`vllm/ir/op.py`)

1. **Extended `@register_op` decorator**

prs.title: [Quant] Consolidate GPTQ: rename gptq_marlin.py to auto_gptq.py
prs.author: chengyinie
prs.body_head:
## Purpose

Consolidate GPTQ quantization by renaming `gptq_marlin.py` to `auto_gptq.py` as requested in #37765.
**Key changes:**
- Renames `gptq_marlin.py` → `auto_gptq.py`
- `GPTQMarlinConfig.get_name()` now returns `"auto_gptq"`
- Adds `override_quantization_method()` to auto-convert models with `quant_method: gptq` to use `auto_gptq`
- Updates all imports across the codebase to use `auto_gptq` module
- Adds `auto_gptq` to overrides list in `model.py`
- Adds `test_auto_gptq.py` for the new quantization method
- Updates `test_gptq_marlin.py` skipif condition to check `auto_gptq` support
- Maintains backward compatibility: `quantization="gptq"` still works (maps to GPTQMarlinConfig)
Closes #37765

## Test Plan
```bash
pytest tests/quantization/test_auto_gptq.py -v -s
pytest tests/quantization/test_gptq_dynamic.py -v -s
pytest tests/quantization/test_lm_head.py -v -s
pytest tests/models/quantization/test_gptq_marlin.py -v -s

## Test Result
 All passed.
---

prs.title: [vLLM IR][Rope] Port RotaryEmbedding and DeepseekScalingRotaryEmbedding to IR Ops
prs.author: wxsIcey
prs.body_head:
## Purpose
Port `RotaryEmbedding` and `DeepseekScalingRotaryEmbedding` to vLLM IR, following https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/issues/38756. For the base `RotaryEmbedding`, keep the `key=None` path out of the IR `maybe_inplace` call and fall back to the existing static implementation.

This preserves cross-layer KV sharing behavior and avoids `torch.library` schema inference failures on optional returns.


## Test Plan
To be tested

## Test Result

prs.title: [ROCm][DX] Clarify collect_env ROCm reporting
prs.author: Bortlesboat
prs.body_head:
﻿## Purpose

Make `collect_env.py` less misleading on ROCm bug reports.

This change does two small things:
1. render ROCm environments under a ROCm-specific GPU info block instead of the current CUDA/NVIDIA wording
2. include ROCm runtime prefixes (`ROCM`, `HIP`, `HSA_`, `MIOPEN`, `RCCL`) in the reported environment variables

That helps with issues like `#39485`, where the environment dump was called out as potentially misleading for a ROCm setup.

This is not duplicating an existing open PR. I checked the current open ROCm PR queue plus issue-body/keyword searches for `collect_env` / ROCm diagnostics before opening this lane. The active ROCm PRs in flight are in device detection, docker/CI, kernels, or model-specific bugfix territory; I did not find an open PR touching `vllm/collect_env.py` for this reporting surface.

AI assistance was used to help draft the patch, but the change is intentionally narrow and I reviewed the touched lines end-to-end.

## Test Plan

- `uv run --with pytest --with regex python -m pytest tests/test_collect_env.py -q --noconftest`
- `uvx ruff check vllm/collect_env.py tests/test_collect_env.py`
- `uvx ruff format --check vllm/collect_env.py tests/test_collect_env.py`
- `uv run --with regex python -m py_compile vllm/collect_env.py tests/test_collect_env.py`
- `git diff --check`

## Test Result

- `tests/test_collect_env.py`: `3 passed`
- `ruff check`: passed
- `ruff format --check`: passed
- `py_compile`: passed
- `git diff --check`: passed

prs.title: `[Perf][MoE][ROCm][Kimi-K2.5] Remove a redundant per-forward-pass dtype conversion of the routing bias parameter in DeepSeek-V2/V3 MoE`
prs.author: xaguilar-amd
prs.body_head: ## Purpose Fixes a redundant per-forward-pass dtype conversion of the routing bias parameter (`e_score_correction_bias`) in the DeepSeek-V2 / DeepSeek-V3 / Kimi-K2 family of MoE models. ### Root cause In `vllm/model_executor/models/deepseek_v2.py`, the routing bias is allocated in `torch.float32`: ```python self.gate.e_score_correction_bias = nn.Parameter( torch.empty(config.n_routed_experts, dtype=torch.float32) ) ``` However the router GEMM (`GateLinear`) produces `gating_output` in a dtype chosen later via `self.gate.set_out_dtype(...)`: - `float32` for monolithic + DeepSeekV3 routing - `bfloat16` for all other paths (DeepSeekV2, Kimi-K2.5, Mistral Large 3, etc.) On the ROCm aiter fused-MoE path, `vllm/model_executor/layers/fused_moe/rocm_aiter_fused_moe.py` then forces the bias to match the gating output dtype every call: ```python # rocm_aiter_fused_moe.py:152-162 if e_score_correction_bias is not None: rocm_aiter_ops.biased_grouped_topk( gating_output, e_score_correction_bias.to(gating_output.dtype), # <-- launches fp32 -> bf16 copy every step topk_weights, topk_ids, ... ) ``` Because `e_score_correction_bias` is a small, constant parameter that never changes after weight loading, doing this conversion once — at construction time — is sufficient. Today it is instead launched as a separate kernel between the gate GEMM and the `biased_grouped_topk` kernel on every MoE layer, on every decode step. ### Impact in a Perfetto trace Profiled on Kimi-K2.5-MXFP4 (ROCm): Before After ### Why fix it in `DeepseekV2MoE` rather than in `rocm_aiter_fused_moe.py` The `.to(gating

prs.title: [ROCm][MLA] validate AITER head counts during selection
prs.author: Bortlesboat
prs.body_head:
## Summary
- reject unsupported dense ROCm AITER MLA head counts during backend selection instead of waiting for backend construction to assert
- keep supported counts like `32` on the AITER path; the remaining bad case on current `main` is unsupported dense counts such as `24`
- add a selector-focused regression that exercises fallback and explicit backend validation without depending on the full ROCm kernel stack

## Why this is not duplicating an existing PR
- I re-ran the duplicate checks for `#38972` and related ROCm MLA keywords before opening this.
- The closest open PR is `#36855`, but that one fixes the sparse MLA `num_heads < 16` path by repeating heads at runtime.
- This PR is about dense ROCm AITER MLA backend selection rejecting unsupported head counts up front so selection can fall back cleanly.

## Testing
- `uv run --no-project --with pytest --with torch --with numpy --with packaging --with pyyaml --with regex --with pydantic --with typing_extensions --with filelock --with cachetools --with blake3 --with msgspec --with msgpack --with cloudpickle --with psutil --with requests --with tqdm --with cbor2 --with pyzmq --with huggingface_hub --with transformers python -m pytest tests/v1/attention/test_rocm_aiter_mla_head_selection.py -q --noconftest`
- `uv run --no-project python -m py_compile vllm/v1/attention/backend.py vllm/platforms/rocm.py vllm/platforms/cuda.py vllm/v1/attention/backends/mla/rocm_aiter_mla.py tests/v1/attention/test_rocm_aiter_mla_head_selection.py`
- `git diff --check`

## AI assistance
- I used AI assistance for drafting and local implementation, and I reviewed the final diff and test results before opening this PR.

Refs #38972.

prs.title: [Frontend][RFC] Rust front-end integration
prs.author: njhill
prs.body_head:
See corresponding RFC for introducing a rust-based alternative front-end process in vLLM https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/issues/40846.

For now we have staged the poc implementation in https://github.com/Inferact/vllm-frontend-rs. This PR contains the logic to integrate it into vLLM.

You can try it out by building this branch:

```shell
# Full install
uv pip install .

# Or use pre-compiled wheel
VLLM_USE_PRECOMPILED=1 uv pip install --editable .
./build_rust.sh

# Run it
VLLM_USE_RUST_FRONTEND=1 vllm serve ...
```

Co-authored with @BugenZhao

prs.title: [ROCm] Use quant_dtype in per_token_quant instead of hardcoded FP8
prs.author: Bortlesboat
prs.body_head:
`_rocm_aiter_per_token_quant_impl` and its fake both accept `quant_dtype` (which can be `torch.int8` or `FP8_DTYPE` per the assert), but hardcode `FP8_DTYPE` for the output tensor allocation. This means passing `torch.int8` would still get an FP8 tensor back.

Simple fix: use `quant_dtype` instead of `FP8_DTYPE` for `torch.empty()`.

Currently all callers pass `FP8_DTYPE` so this hasn't surfaced, but it's a correctness bug if int8 quantization is ever enabled on this path.

No duplicate PRs found (PR #36092 covers other _aiter_ops bugs but not this one). AI assistance was used for code search; all changes reviewed by human submitter.

Signed-off-by: Bortlesboat <bortstheboat@gmail.com>

prs.title: [ROCm][AITER][Bugfix] Disable emulation for MoE
prs.author: heachary
prs.body_head:
## Purpose
PR #39801 introduced a regression for models using the w_mxfp4_a_mxfp4 scheme (e.g. Kimi-K2-Thinking-MXFP4). The _AITER_NATIVE_OCP_MX_SCHEMES tuple only included w_mxfp4, so w_mxfp4_a_mxfp4 was not recognized as a natively supported scheme and fell back to emulation despite aiter's CK MoE kernel supporting it. This PR adds w_mxfp4_a_mxfp4 to the supported set, restoring native execution. 

## Test Plan

[x] Added a unit test to make sure `emulate` is set correctly

## Test Result

prs.title: [Attention Backend] TurboQuant: 2-bit KV cache compression with 4x capacity
prs.author: vibhavagarwal5
prs.body_head:
### Summary

TurboQuant adds **online KV cache compression** to vLLM's v1 attention backend using PolarQuant (WHT rotation + Lloyd-Max scalar quantization) for keys and uniform quantization for values. All quantization happens at store time via fused Triton kernels — no offline calibration, model changes, or weight modifications required. Just set `--kv-cache-dtype turboquant_k8v4`.

### Compression Presets (Qwen3-4B, head_dim=128)

| Preset | Key | Value | Slot (bytes) | Compression | GSM8K | NIAH |
|--------|-----|-------|-------------|-------------|-------|------|
| `turboquant_k8v4` | FP8 (E4M3) | 4-bit uniform | 196 | **2.6x** | 0.860 | 100% |
| `turboquant_4bit_nc` | 4-bit MSE + NC | 4-bit uniform + NC | 136 | **3.8x** | 0.840 | 100% |
| `turboquant_k3v4_nc` | 3-bit MSE + NC | 4-bit uniform + NC | 120 | **4.3x** | 0.780 | 100% |
| `turboquant_3bit_nc` | 3-bit MSE + NC | 3-bit uniform + NC | 104 | **4.9x** | 0.720 | 100% |

Baseline: GSM8K 0.900, NIAH 100%. Measured on Qwen/Qwen3-4B with 5-shot GSM8K (200q) and NIAH (512-32K, 77 probes).

### Performance (Qwen3-4B, 4x RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell, cudagraphs+compile)

#### Throughput (output tok/s)

| Scenario | Baseline | k8v4 | % base | t4nc | % base | k3v4nc | % base | t3nc | % base |
|----------|---------|------|--------|------|--------|--------|--------|------|--------|
| short-decode (128→512) | 8977 | **7113** | **79%** | 6397 | 71% | 6206 | 69% | 6114 | 68% |
| long-prefill (4096→128) | 850 | **811** | **95%** | 766 | 90% | 745 | 88% | 730 | 86% |
| mixed (512→512) | 6618 | **5279** | **80%** | 4829 | 73% | 4584 | 69% | 4491 | 68% |
| high-load (512→128, n=500) | 5633 | **4751** | **84%** | 4456 | 79% | 4337 | 77% | 4240 | 75% |
| very-long-prefill (8192→64) | 233 | **234** | **100%** | 224 | 96% | 220 | 94% | 216 | 93% |
| decode-heavy (64→1024) | 8304 | **6521** | **79%** | 5887 | 71% | 5650 | 68% | 5430 | 65% |

#### TPOT (ms) — lower is better

| Scenario | baseline | k8v4 |
